page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for matrix math acceleration.

Benchmark evaluations place the D16s_v7 in the top tier for single-threaded gzip compression at 35.53 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.66 GB/sec. It also achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small models at 338.07 tokens/sec. Most other metrics fall within the average category, including a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 16553.78, a PassMark CPU score of 27099.51, and a static web serving throughput of 30.03 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is measured at 132.44 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 54.19 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, meaning the instance relies entirely on external Premium Storage. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency by allowing users to scale storage independently of compute resources, avoiding the premium associated with bundled local NVMe drives. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks that leverage its matrix and vector instruction sets.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache, but lacks bundled local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation. Most other compute and memory benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 27099.51 and static web serving throughput of 30.03 GB/sec, rank in the average tier. Lacking local storage, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring decoupled storage scaling. It is suited for database operations, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory-to-core ratios, advanced matrix extensions, and top-tier single-threaded compression capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and a 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and moderate inference workloads
